Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Pedro Echarte, Esq. Mike Roth, Esq. 305-446-5700 The Haggard Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Damali Taylor, Esq. Ryne Cannon, Esq. 415-984-8928 for Defendant.
Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR#13293, 415-274-9977, calendar-la@veritext.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Ms. Taylor contests and presents arguments; prejudice, timeliness, cites case law, private interest consideration. The Court comments, poses questions. Court and Counsel have a discussion. Mr. Echarte responds to Plaintiff's arguments; untimely, has not received anything and counsel has not cooperated, might be prejudiced if held in Mexico, property not inspected, employment records and non-economic damages, problem with the platform. Ms. Taylor offers a brief rebuttal; case should have been dismissed outright. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants Airbnb, Inc., Airbnb Payments, Inc. and Airbnb Travel, LLC's (collectively "Airbnb") Motion To Dismiss Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens.

Taken Under Submission.***

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR#13293, 415-274-9977, calendar-la@veritext.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***

Off Calendar May-15-2025 Continued To Jul-09-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Pedro P. Echarte III, Esq. 305-446-5700 The Haggard Law Firm for Plaintiff. (In-person)
Damali Taylor, 415-984-8928 O'Melveny for Defendant Airbnb et al.
Court reporter: David Salyer, CSR #4410, 323-497-1441, davesal55@gmail.com

RULING - The matter is NOT contested/Discussed.The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANTs AIRBNB, INC, AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC.,and AIRBNB TRAVEL, LLC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendants Airbnb Payments, Inc. and Airbnb Travel LLC's Motion to Strike is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.

"Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof..." (CCP 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (CCP 436.) "In the construction of a pleading, for the purpose of determining its effect, its allegations must be liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (CCP 452.)

As to the prayer for decedent's lost earnings, Plaintiff concedes that the request is improper. (Opposition, 5:27-28.) Thus, the court grants the motion and the prayer for decedent's lost earnings is ordered stricken without leave to amend.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: David Salyer, CSR #4410, 323-497-1441, davesal55@gmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)
